---
title: "Template Engine — Standards & Requirements"
subtitle: "Templated Document Processing, Generation, and Verification"
author:
  - name: "Shahin Mohammadi"
    affiliation: "Cytognosis Foundation"
date: 2026-03-02
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
version: "1.0"
status: "Draft"

format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    self-contained: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    papersize: letter
    fontsize: 11pt
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    pdf-engine: lualatex
  docx:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  gfm:
    toc: true

crossref:
  tbl-prefix: "Table"

bibliography: references.bib
---

# Purpose {#sec-purpose}

This document defines the formal requirements for selecting a standards-based, open-source toolchain for **templated document processing, generation, and verification**. The selected tool(s) must support:

- Creating structured documents from **templates** (layout/style specification) and **content** (material/data)
- Producing high-quality outputs across **multiple formats** (HTML, PDF, DOCX, EPUB)
- Using **text-only, VCS-friendly** source files for both templates and content
- Rendering **scientific/technical content** (equations, diagrams, code)
- Integrating seamlessly into **AI-agent-driven automation workflows**

::: {.callout-note}
## Decision Context
This specification was developed after a comparative analysis of 10 candidate tools. See the companion document: [Template Engine Comparative Analysis — Top 10](template_engine_comparative_analysis_top10.md).
:::

---

# Requirements Classification {#sec-classification}

Requirements are classified into three tiers with weighted scoring:

| Tier | Label | Multiplier | Meaning |
|------|-------|:----------:|---------|
| **R** | Required | ×3 | Candidate is **disqualified** without this capability |
| **SP** | Strongly Preferred | ×2 | **Significant penalty** for absence |
| **P** | Preferred | ×1 | **Positive differentiator** when present |

: Requirements tier definitions and scoring weights {#tbl-tiers}

---

# Requirements {#sec-requirements}

## Licensing & Ecosystem {#sec-licensing}

| ID | Tier | Requirement |
|----|:----:|-------------|
| **R-1** | **SP** | The tool/library **shall be open-source** with an OSI-approved license (e.g., MIT, Apache-2, GPL). |
| **R-2** | **P** | The tool **should be widely adopted** with strong community support, measurable via GitHub stars (>1k), active maintenance, and ecosystem plugins/extensions. |

: Licensing & ecosystem requirements {#tbl-req-licensing}

## Input Formats & Version Control {#sec-input}

| ID | Tier | Requirement |
|----|:----:|-------------|
| **R-3** | **P** | **Shall support multiple, widely accepted input formats** for both template specification and content material (e.g., Markdown, YAML frontmatter, Jupyter Notebooks, reStructuredText). |
| **R-4** | **SP** | Both the **template specification and content material shall be text-only formats** (e.g., Markdown + YAML, not binary formats) to enable tracking with standard VCS tools such as `git`. |
| **R-5** | **P** | If only a single/few input formats are supported, there **shall exist open-source tools** (e.g., Pandoc) that can convert to/from other standard formats seamlessly. |

: Input format & VCS requirements {#tbl-req-input}

## Media & Visual Content {#sec-media}

| ID | Tier | Requirement |
|----|:----:|-------------|
| **R-6** | **R** | **Shall support embedded images** in documents (PNG, JPEG, SVG, PDF). |
| **R-7** | **P** | **Should provide options to adjust size, position, and style** of embedded images (width, height, alignment, captions, alt-text). |
| **R-8** | **P** | If image styling is not natively supported, preprocessing via external tools (e.g., ImageMagick) is acceptable but counts as a negative signal. |

: Media & visual content requirements {#tbl-req-media}

## Scientific & Technical Content {#sec-scientific}

| ID | Tier | Requirement |
|----|:----:|-------------|
| **R-9** | **SP** | **Shall support mathematical equations** via standard LaTeX math notation, rendered correctly in all output formats (HTML via MathJax/KaTeX, PDF via LaTeX, DOCX via equation objects). |
| **R-10** | **SP** | **Shall support diagram generation** from text-based specifications at compile/render time. Must support at least one of: Mermaid, Graphviz (DOT), or PlantUML. |

: Scientific & technical content requirements {#tbl-req-scientific}

## Output Formats {#sec-output}

| ID | Tier | Requirement |
|----|:----:|-------------|
| **R-11** | **P** | **Shall support multiple output formats**, including at minimum: HTML, PDF, DOCX, and at least one additional format (EPUB, Markdown, presentations). |
| **R-12** | **P** | If only a few output formats are natively supported: (a) there must exist OSS tools to chain conversions, **and** (b) the natively supported format(s) must be text-based/git-trackable (e.g., Markdown → PDF via Pandoc + LaTeX). |

: Output format requirements {#tbl-req-output}

## AI & Automation Integration {#sec-ai}

| ID | Tier | Requirement |
|----|:----:|-------------|
| **R-13** | **P** | The tool **should be widely adopted by LLMs/AI agents** — either via native knowledge, standard skills/plugins, or MCP servers — enabling seamless integration into multi-agent automation workflows. |

: AI & automation integration requirements {#tbl-req-ai}

## Dynamic Content & Computation {#sec-dynamic}

| ID | Tier | Requirement |
|----|:----:|-------------|
| **R-14** | **P** | **Should support dynamic content generation**, including execution of code cells (Python, R, Julia) and inline rendering of their outputs (figures, tables, text) in the final document. |

: Dynamic content requirements {#tbl-req-dynamic}

---

# Evaluation Criteria {#sec-evaluation}

## Scoring Scale

Each candidate is scored **0–3** per requirement:

| Score | Meaning |
|:-----:|---------|
| **3** | Fully supported natively, out-of-the-box |
| **2** | Supported with minimal configuration or first-party plugin |
| **1** | Achievable via third-party OSS tools or chaining |
| **0** | Not supported or prohibitively difficult |

: Per-requirement scoring scale {#tbl-scoring}

## Weighted Aggregation

Each per-requirement score is multiplied by the tier weight (see @tbl-tiers), then summed across all requirements.

**Maximum possible score:**

$$
\text{Max} = \underbrace{(1 \times 3 \times 3)}_{\text{Required}} + \underbrace{(4 \times 2 \times 3)}_{\text{Strongly Preferred}} + \underbrace{(7 \times 1 \times 3)}_{\text{Preferred}} = 9 + 24 + 21 = 54
$$ {#eq-max-score}

## Summary by Tier

| Tier | Count | Weight | Max Contribution |
|------|:-----:|:------:|:----------------:|
| Required | 1 | ×3 | 9 |
| Strongly Preferred | 4 | ×2 | 24 |
| Preferred | 7 | ×1 | 21 |
| **Total** | **12** | | **54** |

: Score contribution by tier {#tbl-score-summary}

::: {.callout-important}
## Disqualification Rule
Any candidate scoring **0** on a Required (R) tier requirement is automatically disqualified, regardless of total score.
:::

---

# Known Candidates {#sec-candidates}

The following tools were pre-identified for evaluation:

1. **Quarto** — Open-source scientific/technical publishing system (Posit/Pandoc-based). Partial positive bias due to widespread data science adoption.
2. **Pandoc** — Universal document converter ("Swiss Army knife" of markup).
3. **Typst** — Modern typesetting system, LaTeX alternative.
4. **Jupyter Book** — Publication-quality books from computational content.
5. **Sphinx** — Python documentation generator.
6. **R Markdown / Bookdown** — R-based literate programming (predecessor to Quarto).
7. **Docusaurus** — Meta's documentation website framework.
8. **Hugo** — Fastest static site generator (Go).
9. **mdBook** — Markdown-based book builder (Rust).
10. **Markdoc** — Stripe's Markdown documentation framework.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Excluded Candidates
**Docmosis** was considered but excluded: it is a commercial (proprietary) product, failing requirement R-1 (open-source, strongly preferred).
:::

---

# Deliverables {#sec-deliverables}

This requirements process produces the following artifacts:

| # | Artifact | Path | Format |
|:-:|----------|------|--------|
| 1 | Requirements specification (this document) | `standards/template_engine_requirements.qmd` | `.qmd` |
| 2 | Quarto format specification | `standards/template_engine_quarto.md` | `.md` |
| 3 | Comparative analysis — top 10 | `standards/template_engine_comparative_analysis_top10.md` | `.md` |
| 4 | Requirements document template | `templates/requirements/requirements_template.qmd` | `.qmd` |

: Deliverables produced from this requirements process {#tbl-deliverables}
